2606.29892.title: Trust Your Instincts: Confidence-Driven Test-Time RL for Vision-Language-Action Models
2606.29892.paper_summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become indispensable for pushing Vision-Language-Action Models (VLAs) beyond static imitation learning. However, existing RL methods typically require external environmental feedback, relying on predefined success signals to guide policy updates. In this work, we show that VLA models possess useful internal evaluative capabilities: in discrete-action VLAs, trajectories with higher generation confidence are significantly more likely to succeed. Based on this observation, we introduce T^2VLA (Test-time VLA), an architecture-agnostic test-time RL framework that enables VLA models to achieve self-bootstrapping policy improvement. Instead of relying on external rewards, T^2VLA leverages trajectory-level similarity to high-confidence expert demonstrations as an intrinsic reward signal. In addition, we propose a Confidence-Driven Dual Expert Bootstrapping mechanism, which dynamically balances a Local Pseudo-Expert for exploration and a Global Expert Pool for training stability. Extensive experiments on the LIBERO and RoboTwin benchmarks show that T^2VLA consistently outperforms supervised baselines and approaches oracle RL performance with ground-truth rewards, achieving effective improvement without external reward feedback. Furthermore, T^2VLA adapts to distinct VLA paradigms, including both OpenVLA-OFT and the pi series.
2606.29892.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習（RL）已成為推動視覺-語言-行動模型（VLA）超越靜態模仿學習的不可或缺的工具。<lb/>然而，現有的RL方法通常需要外部環境反饋，依賴預定義的成功信號來指導策略更新。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們展示了VLA模型擁有有用的內部評估能力：在離散行動的VLA中，具有更高生成信心的軌跡成功的可能性顯著更高。<lb/>基於這一觀察，我們引入了T^2VLA（測試時VLA），這是一個架構無關的測試時RL框架，使VLA模型能夠實現自我引導的策略改進。<lb/>T^2VLA不依賴外部獎勵，而是利用與高信心專家示範的軌跡級相似性作為內在獎勵信號。<lb/>此外，我們提出了一種基於信心的雙專家自引導機制，該機制動態平衡探索的局部偽專家和訓練穩定性的全局專家池。<lb/>在LIBERO和RoboTwin基準上的廣泛實驗顯示，T^2VLA始終超越監督基線，並在獲得真實獎勵的情況下接近oracle RL性能，實現了在沒有外部獎勵反饋的情況下的有效改進。<lb/>此外，T^2VLA適應於不同的VLA範式，包括OpenVLA-OFT和pi系列。
2606.29892.author: Siyao Chen et.al.
2606.29892.authors: Siyao Chen, Jiakang Yuan, Jiaxin Wang, Tao Chen
